summary: Full backend + frontend testing for Evenda. 18/18 backend pytest cases pass. All 9 frontend flows pass: home (hero + 9 category pills + search), search page (cards/pills/sort + q filter + category filter), entry detail, admin login, contribute form submit (auth-gated, creates entry & redirects to /entry/:id), logout (gates contribute), register (auto-login). Backend correctly excludes Mongo _id, uses httpOnly cookies (samesite=none, secure=true), enforces 401 for unauthenticated POST /api/entries, 409 for duplicate register, 401 for wrong password.

minor.issue: Seed entry count grows across test runs (saw 16 cards) because POST /api/entries inserts persist; no cleanup. Test data 'TEST_term_*' remains in DB. Acceptable for now but consider a cleanup or test-only soft delete endpoint.

issues: Console shows benign 401 from GET /api/auth/me when user is not logged in; frontend handles it but noisy in DevTools. Consider a 204/empty-user response or suppressing the log in axios interceptor.

action_items: (Optional) Suppress noisy /api/auth/me 401 console error for unauthenticated visitors (e.g., return 200 + null user, or silence axios error log).
(Optional) Add a cleanup mechanism for TEST_-prefixed entries created by automated tests.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 378-line file — acceptable for MVP but consider splitting auth/entries routers into modules as it grows.
JWT_SECRET is read via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] — good (fails fast). Verified via tests.
Bcrypt hashes use $2b$ format (verified by login flow). seed_admin idempotently re-hashes on password change — playbook compliant.

CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins falling back to ['*'] when CORS_ORIGINS unset; per CORS spec this combination is invalid in browsers. Production should set explicit origin via FRONTEND_URL/CORS_ORIGINS (currently works because preview env likely sets it).
create_entry returns Entry by filtering out 'contributor_id' — clean, but consider including it in the model for traceability.
Search uses regex on user input without escaping ($regex special chars) — minor: typing '.*' or '(' could throw or match unexpectedly. Consider re.escape on q.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout). Not blocking but worth adding.
seed_data_creation: Seed runs on startup and inserted 15 entries + admin user. Verified via GET /api/entries (>=15) and admin login.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/ -v. Frontend testids: hero-* on Home, search-input/search-submit/category-filter-{cat}/sort-{val}/entry-card-{id} on Search, entry-* on detail, login-email/login-password/login-submit on Login, register-name/email/password/submit on Register, contribute-input-{key}/contribute-submit on Contribute, nav-contribute/nav-logout/nav-user-name in Header. Note: Search now shows 16+ entries because previous tests added entries; this is expected.
